Title: ZmNRT1.1B (ZmNPF6.6) determines nitrogen use efficiency via regulation of nitrate transport and signalling in maize

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: ND101; B73; PH6WC/PH4CV hybrid background XY335
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NPF/POT nitrate transporter family; NRT1.1/NPF6.6 transceptor-like transporter
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed across diverse tissues; 24–1600-fold higher than ZmNRT1.1C; induced by nitrate but not ammonium; downregulated by N deprivation; peaks around 6 h after nitrate resupply
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root uptake system and root-to-shoot translocation stream
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low versus high nitrate; nitrate starvation/resupply; field low-N, moderate-N, high-N fertilizer conditions
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes high-affinity nitrate uptake, root-to-shoot nitrate translocation, nitrate signalling, biomass accumulation, nitrogen content, NUE and grain yield; overexpression improves yield under N-limiting fields
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Loss of function reduced shoot/root biomass and N content, especially under high nitrate. 2. 15N assays showed reduced high-affinity nitrate influx and reduced root-to-shoot nitrate translocation. 3. Required for full induction of nitrate-responsive genes and for nitrate-triggered nuclear retention of ZmNLP3.1. 4. Overexpression increased low-N tolerance and grain yield in hybrid backgrounds under low/moderate N.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; transgenic overexpression; 15N-labelled nitrate influx and translocation assays; qRT-PCR; RNA-seq; subcellular localization in protoplasts; protoplast nitrate-response/nuclear shuttling assay; field trials
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ZmNLP3.1 (functional signalling module; no direct physical interaction shown)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant growth, nitrogen acquisition/utilization efficiency, and grain yield under limited N supply
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No yield advantage under high-N conditions; introgression barely impacted plant height, suggesting low lodging risk
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding N-efficient maize; overexpression in elite hybrid XY335 improved yield under low/moderate N and maintained yield under ~30% N reduction; useful for reducing fertilizer input
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although discussed as a transporter/sensor (transceptor), the paper emphasizes both transport and signalling roles rather than enzymatic catalysis or classic TF regulation; therefore classified as Common_Gene.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NPF/POT nitrate transporter family; tandem duplicated NRT1.1-like proteoform
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Much lower expression than ZmNRT1.1B; induced by high nitrate and high ammonium; downregulated by N deprivation; induced by nitrate and ammonium resupply
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low versus high nitrate; ammonium versus nitrate supply; N starvation/resupply
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No significant effect on seedling growth, biomass, or N accumulation under tested nitrate regimes
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tandem duplicate of ZmNRT1.1B with divergent expression and localization. 2. Localizes to endomembrane-like structures rather than plasma membrane. 3. Loss of function caused no obvious growth or N-accumulation phenotype under tested nitrate conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; transient subcellular localization; hydroponic phenotyping
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited value suggested in this study because no clear NUE phenotype was detected
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a direct experimental subject in Results, although evidence indicates it is not functionally decisive for NUE under the tested conditions.

Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm and nucleus; nitrate promotes nuclear retention
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transcript level not significantly altered by ZmNRT1.1B mutation under low or high nitrate; protein redistributes to nucleus upon nitrate supply in WT
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation associated with nitrate-triggered cytoplasmic-to-nuclear shuttling/nuclear retention
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ZmNR1.1; ZmNR1.2; ZmNRT2.1; cytokinin biosynthesis genes such as ZmIPT6 and ZmCYP735A2/"ZmCYPA" as reported; carbon metabolism genes including ZmPGD3, ZmMDH8, ZmFNR1; broad nitrate-responsive gene sets
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positively regulates nitrate-responsive, cytokinin-biosynthesis, and carbon-metabolism genes; mutation reduces their nitrate induction
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Nitrate signalling; nitrate assimilation; coordination of nitrogen, carbon and cytokinin-related metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator of nitrate-mediated growth and transcriptional response; functions with ZmNRT1.1B as a core signalling module
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Whole-plant nitrogen accumulation via root and shoot
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports plant growth, biomass accumulation, grain yield, and NUE under nitrate nutrition
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrate supply versus KCl control; low versus high nitrate; field low-N/high-N conditions
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator required for nitrate-mediated biomass accumulation, N accumulation, and grain yield; loss of function impairs nitrate signalling and reduces yield
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR mutants showed reduced biomass and N accumulation, especially under high nitrate. 2. RNA-seq showed ZmNLP3.1 controls a large fraction of nitrate-responsive genes overlapping with ZmNRT1.1B targets. 3. Nitrate induces nuclear retention of ZmNLP3.1, and this depends on ZmNRT1.1B. 4. Field mutants displayed reduced biomass and yield under both low- and high-N.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; nitrate-triggered nuclear localization assay in protoplasts; hydroponic phenotyping; field trials
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ZmNRT1.1B (functional upstream regulator in signalling module)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions in ZmNRT1.1B-ZmNLP3.1 nitrate signalling module; no direct stable protein complex biochemically demonstrated
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function strongly reduces biomass and yield; overexpression/breeding value not tested in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising nitrate-signalling target for future breeding evaluation, though field value of engineered lines remains to be tested
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also referred to as ZmNLP8 in the summary. The paper supports a signalling role via nitrate-dependent nuclear retention rather than reporting direct DNA-binding assays in this study.
